name: Scarlatos et al. Real Tutoring Dialogue Comparison Extract
population_or_context: Turn-level simulated students in math tutoring dialogues compared with real student turns.
sample_size: The source benchmarks prompt-based and post-trained simulated-student methods on a real math tutoring dialogue dataset; exact dataset composition is not restated in this staged record.
methods: Extracted the real tutoring dialogue comparator and turn-level fidelity metrics for simulated-student methods.
education_context: Math tutoring dialogue simulation for intelligent tutoring systems.
response_origin: Prompted and post-trained LLMs generate student turns that are evaluated against ground-truth student turns from real tutoring dialogues.
rubric_label_space: Dialogue-act, correctness, error-equivalence, knowledge-acquisition, linguistic-similarity, and tutor-response metrics.
label_source: Ground-truth dialogue turns, LLM-assisted annotations, automated metrics, and human evaluation provide comparison signals.
generation_model: Prompting methods use GPT-4.1 and GPT-5 mini; post-training methods use Llama 3.2 3B and Llama 3.1 8B student simulators.
generation_method: Zero-shot, persona, in-context, reasoning, and oracle prompting are compared with SFT and DPO post-training workflows.
prompt_strategy: Prompting variants include zero-shot, OCEAN persona, in-context learning, reasoning, and oracle summaries.
evaluation_method: Turn-level metrics compare simulated and real student dialogue acts, correctness, error equivalence, knowledge, text similarity, ROUGE-L, and induced tutor responses.
scorer_use: Dialogue simulator benchmarking; not automated scorer validation.
real_response_comparator: Real math tutoring dialogue turns provide the ground-truth comparator.
reported_metrics: Table 1 reports DPO (Llama 3.1 8B) at Acts 0.6840, Knowledge 0.8787, Cos. Sim. 0.7390, ROUGE-L 0.3212, Tutor Resp. 0.2039, while Zero-Shot (GPT-4.1) reports Acts 0.4998, Knowledge 0.8078, Cos. Sim. 0.5460, ROUGE-L 0.1648, Tutor Resp. 0.1911.
limitations: The comparison is dialogue-turn specific and does not directly transfer to essays or item-response simulation.
Method packages differ by model family and training workflow, so causal claims should remain bounded.
The paper reports remaining gaps, especially for reproducing specific student errors.
